description_long: This object is a vintage tabletop radio receiver with a rectangular wooden body and a glossy, dark brown finish. The front panel features a large speaker grille at the top, a central frequency display with AM and SW bands, and two prominent gold-colored knobs on either side for tuning and volume control. The frequency display is marked with various radio bands and is flanked by turquoise accent panels. The sides and back are smooth, polished wood with no visible controls or openings, emphasizing the classic, streamlined design. The bottom edge of the front panel has several white push buttons, likely for band selection or preset functions. The overall appearance is reminiscent of mid-20th-century radios, combining both decorative and functional elements.
description: A vintage wooden radio receiver with a glossy finish, gold knobs, a large speaker grille, and a detailed frequency display on the front.
description_view_0: Front view showing the speaker grille, frequency display, gold tuning and volume knobs, and white push buttons.
description_view_1: Left side view displaying the smooth, polished wooden surface with no visible controls or features.
description_view_2: Rear view showing a flat, uninterrupted wooden panel, highlighting the radio's solid construction.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, with a continuous, glossy wooden finish and no controls.
